channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Nacon Connect 2026 Delayed to May Amid "Difficult Economic Environment"
item.content:encoded:
Nacon has announced that its annual Nacon Connect presentation, which was originally set to air this month, has been delayed to May in order to allow the company to focus its resources on more immediate releases.

In a press release, Nacon says that the show, which was to air in just a couple of days, will instead be taking place in May. No new specific date has been announced, so we'll have to wait to find out exactly when in May we can see the show.

Nacon blames the delay on a "difficult economic environment" for the studio, and it's hard to disagree; last week, the publisher filed for insolvency, claiming that it no longer had the assets to meet its liabilities.

Rather than airing the showcase this week, Nacon says it will use the time to "focus [its] resources on upcoming releases and the development of [its] current games", as well as to "polish [its] projects" and ensure that what's being shown "showcase[s] the work of [its] studios in the best possible way".

Before the Nacon Connect arrives, Nacon has promised "communications" regarding new and upcoming releases like GreedFall: The Dying World, Dragonkin: The Banished, and Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss, at least one of which was to be featured during this week's presentation.

In addition to Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss, this week's show would also have featured games like The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu (yep, another Lovecraftian horror game), as well as Endurance Motorsport Series and more.

Whether we'd have seen anything about the indefinitely-delayed Terminator: Survivors is less clear, but perhaps the additional time afforded by this delay will allow Nacon to polish the game up to a state that makes it fit to be shown.

The 2026 Nacon Connect show is now scheduled to arrive sometime in May. Stay tuned for more on this and all other things Nacon.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/nacon-connect-2026-delayed-to-may
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Old World Empires of the Indus DLC Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Developer Mohawk Games and publisher Hooded Horse have announced the release date for the upcoming Old World DLC Empires of the Indus, which brings new playable nations and more at the end of the month.

As revealed in a Steam post, Empires of the Indus arrives on March 30th. Its first major new feature is three extra playable nations in the form of the Mauryans, the Yuezhi, and the Tamilakam, each representing a major player "across a 600-year period of upheaval and opportunity".

As well as these new nations, you can also look forward to a new tribe, the Huns, and they're accompanied by new unique events that cover the "emergence" of the Huns' "most famous son", Attila.

Two new religions, Hinduism and Buddhism, will be added to the game in Empires of the Indus, and all three new nations will begin as Hindu nations, with the Mauryans able to establish Buddhism "through a unique project".

Four new Wonders will be introduced in the new DLC, too, in addition to a new Jungle vegetation type, more than 200 new events, and plenty more. In short, it's set to be a pretty sizeable DLC, so if you love Old World but could go in for more, this one's for you.

If you're unfamiliar with Old World, it's a Civilization-style 4X strategy game, and it's the brainchild of Civilization IV designer Soren Johnson. Like Civ, the game gives you control of your own empire over multiple generations, but it's also a more character-focused affair with a Crusader Kings flavor to it.

Publishing for the game is handled by Hooded Horse, which made a splash recently when its head Tim Bender revealed that the studio has a no-generative-AI clause written into its developer contracts.

You'll be able to check out Old World's Empires of the Indus DLC when it arrives on PC via Steam on March 30th. The base game is available to check out right now on PC as well.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/old-world-empires-of-indus-dlc-release-date
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Starfield PS5 Release Date Seemingly Leaked for April
item.content:encoded:
It looks as though a Starfield PS5 release date has been leaked, and if the leak is correct, then Bethesda's open-world space RPG is heading for Sony's console in just a few weeks.

This report comes from Dealabs' bilibil-kun, who's previously leaked the existence of the PS5 Pro, as well as several past PlayStation Plus lineups and much more. In general, bilibil-kun is a pretty reliable leaker, so this Starfield PS5 release date is probably accurate.

Additionally, bilibil-kun says that Starfield preorders on PS5 are likely to open somewhere around March 17th or March 18th, so that's probably also when the official announcement will happen, assuming that this leak doesn't bump Bethesda's plans up in the calendar.

Unfortunately, if you've already played Starfield and you were hoping for some sweeping changes before checking out the game again, that's probably not going to happen.

Back in February, Bethesda's Todd Howard told Kinda Funny (via Kotaku) that the next major Starfield update is "not Starfield 2.0" and that it's unlikely to change the minds of those who "bounced off it, or found it boring in places". Thanks for the heads up, Todd.

It's not clear whether Starfield's PS5 release will include its Shattered Space expansion, but bilibil-kun says that two editions of the game, a Standard Edition and a Premium Edition, will be available, so it's possible the DLC will be included with the latter.

We do, however, know that Starfield will be getting a physical release on PS5 (again, assuming this leak is accurate), so you'll probably want to make some shelf space for it if you're intending to pick it up.

For the time being, you can grab Starfield on PC and Xbox Series X|S, and it's also available as part of certain Game Pass subscription tiers. Make sure to read our Erren's review of the game from back at launch to find out what she thought of it!

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/starfield-ps5-release-date-seemingly-leaked
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nintendo Indie World March 2026 Showcase Set for Tomorrow
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo has announced a brand new Nintendo Indie World showcase for tomorrow, so get ready to learn about some indie titles heading to Switch 2 and Switch in the near future.

This news was revealed via Nintendo's social media, and the company is promising "roughly 15 minutes of news and updates on indie games coming to Nintendo Switch 2 and Nintendo Switch", which sounds about right for an Indie World show.

The duration of the show is pretty closely in keeping with previous Indie World showcases, so you can probably expect pretty much the same format as always; a cavalcade of indie games for your perusal, possibly broken up by some endearing (if slightly overeager) presenter banter.

Since this is an Indie World show, you should not, of course, expect any news on first-party Nintendo titles, so don't go into it thinking you're going to see the next mainline Zelda or Mario title; this ain't that kind of show, bub.

We should, however, get new looks at upcoming Switch 2 and Switch indies. Upcoming titles for the systems include the likes of magical life sim Witchbrook, Yacht Club's top-down Zelda homage Mina the Hollower, and viking survival game Valheim, to name but a few.

The presentation will also likely include some surprises we aren't expecting. I, personally, would love to see a Switch 2 release of Black Tabby's indie visual novel Scarlet Hollow, especially since the studio's other game Slay the Princess has enjoyed such success.

I'd also like to hear from cyberpunk restaurant management sim-slash-slice of life game Nivalis, which has been delayed a couple of times. This is just speculation, though; anything could be announced, and it's impossible to guess exactly what we're in for.

Whatever Nintendo has in store for us, we'll find out during tomorrow's Nintendo Indie World showcase, which will air at 6am Pacific/9am Eastern/2pm GMT tomorrow. Stay tuned for everything that's revealed during that show.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
